content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/mac/Documents/EaglePoint/TASK-ridecircle/docs/test-coverage.md", "content": "# RideCircle Carpool Marketplace -- Test Coverage Strategy

## Philosophy

Risk-first testing: prioritize tests for business logic that is hardest to verify by static review, has the highest blast radius if broken, or involves complex state transitions.

## Risk Tiers

### Tier 1 -- Critical (Must have before any deployment)

These areas have strict business rules where a bug means data corruption, security breach, or broken core workflow.

| Area | Risk | Test Type | What to Test |
|------|------|-----------|--------------|
| Order state machine | Invalid transitions corrupt data; financial/trust implications | Unit | Every valid transition succeeds; every invalid transition is rejected; auto-expire at exactly 30 min; free cancel window at exactly 5 min; in-progress cancel blocked; dispute window at exactly 72 hours |
| Authentication | Broken auth = security breach | Unit + API | Session creation/validation/expiry; token creation/hashing/rotation/expiry; invalid credentials rejected; expired tokens rejected |
| Authorization (RBAC + object-level) | Broken authz = data leak or privilege escalation | API | Each role can only access permitted endpoints; object ownership enforced (user A cannot edit user B's listing); admin-only endpoints reject non-admins |
| Password handling | Plaintext storage = catastrophic | Unit | Passwords are hashed (bcrypt/argon2); plaintext never stored or logged; hash comparison works |
| Signed URL validation | Broken = unauthorized media access | Unit + API | Valid signature accepted; expired signature rejected; tampered signature rejected; missing params rejected |

### Tier 2 -- High (Must have before feature complete)

Business logic that is complex but whose failures are more visible and less catastrophic.

| Area | Risk | Test Type | What to Test |
|------|------|-----------|--------------|
| Listing workflow | Wrong status transitions; draft/publish confusion | Unit | All valid transitions; invalid transitions rejected; bulk close only affects eligible listings; version created on each edit |
| Review validation | Bad data persisted; rate limit bypass | Unit + API | Rating 1-5 enforced; text length 1-1000; file count max 5; file size limits; rate limit (3/hour) enforced and returns 429 |
| Content moderation flags | Offensive content published; false positives | Unit | Sensitive word detection (case-insensitive, word-boundary); duplicate text detection at threshold; file fingerprint match; credibility score computation |
| Media upload | Oversized files; wrong types accepted | API | File type validation; file size rejection; correct MIME type check; SHA-256 hash computed |
| Hotlink protection | Media accessible without auth | API | Missing referrer + no signature = 403; wrong referrer = 403; valid signed URL + valid referrer = 200 |

### Tier 3 -- Medium (Should have before release)

Features that are important but whose failure modes are less severe.

| Area | Risk | Test Type | What to Test |
|------|------|-----------|--------------|
| Search and suggestions | Poor UX but not data loss | API | Keyword search returns matching results; highlights present; suggestions returned for partial input; did-you-mean for known typos; no-results fallback shows recent listings |
| Credibility score | Wrong weighting affects ratings display | Unit | Score computation with various inputs; age factor < 14 days = 0.5; completion factor correct; pattern penalties applied; score < 0.3 flags review |
| Data governance nightly jobs | Stale data; metric drift | Unit | Dedup removes duplicate events in window; missing-value fill corrects counters; lineage records created; quality metrics computed correctly |
| Pagination | Missing data on pages | API | Page 1 returns correct count; last page correct; out-of-range page returns empty data (not error) |
| Audit logging | Missing audit trail | Integration | CRUD operations on listings/orders/reviews create audit log entries; PII is masked by default; unmasked only with permission |

### Tier 4 -- Low (Nice to have)

| Area | Risk | Test Type | What to Test |
|------|------|-----------|--------------|
| Watermark application | Visual quality | Manual | Watermark visible but not obstructive; correct position; transparency correct |
| 12-hour time format | Display issue | Unit | AM/PM parsing and display correct; midnight and noon edge cases |
| Version diff | Display issue | Unit | Diff correctly identifies changed fields; unchanged fields not shown |

## Test Structure

```
repo/
  unit_tests/
    auth/
      LoginTest.php
      TokenTest.php
      PasswordHashTest.php
    listing/
      ListingWorkflowTest.php
      ListingVersionTest.php
      ListingValidationTest.php
    order/
      OrderStateMachineTest.php
      OrderExpiryTest.php
      OrderCancelTest.php
      OrderDisputeTest.php
    review/
      ReviewValidationTest.php
      ReviewRateLimitTest.php
    moderation/
      SensitiveWordTest.php
      DuplicateDetectionTest.php
      CredibilityScoreTest.php
    media/
      SignedUrlTest.php
      FileValidationTest.php
      FingerprintTest.php
    governance/
      DedupJobTest.php
      QualityMetricsTest.php
      LineageTest.php
  API_tests/
    AuthApiTest.php
    ListingApiTest.php
    OrderApiTest.php
    ReviewApiTest.php
    MediaApiTest.php
    ModerationApiTest.php
    GovernanceApiTest.php
    AuditApiTest.php
    PermissionApiTest.php
```

## Coverage Targets

| Tier | Target | Rationale |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Tier 1 | 100% of stated rules | These are non-negotiable business constraints |
| Tier 2 | >90% of happy + error paths | Complex logic with meaningful failure modes |
| Tier 3 | >70% happy paths | Important but failure is more visible/less catastrophic |
| Tier 4 | Manual verification | Cost of automation exceeds risk |

## Running Tests

```bash
# All tests
./run_tests.sh

# Unit tests only
./run_tests.sh unit

# API tests only (requires running backend + MySQL)
./run_tests.sh api
```

## Key Test Principles

1. **Order state machine tests are the single most important test suite.** Every boundary condition (exactly 30 min, exactly 5 min, exactly 72 hours) must be tested with time mocking.
2. **Authorization tests must cover negative cases.** It is not enough to verify that an admin can access admin endpoints; tests must also verify that a regular user receives 403.
3. **Rate limit tests must use time mocking** to avoid flaky tests that depend on wall-clock timing.
4. **Media tests must use real files** (small fixtures) to verify MIME type detection and hash computation, not mocked file objects.
5. **No external dependencies in tests.** Tests must run fully offline, matching the production constraint.
"}

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/mac/Documents/EaglePoint/TASK-ridecircle/docs/reviewer-notes.md", "content": "# RideCircle Carpool Marketplace -- Reviewer Notes

## Purpose

This document guides a static reviewer to the most important evidence in the repository. It identifies where to look, what to verify, and which risks need the closest attention.

---

## 1. Where to Start

| Priority | What to Review | Where to Find It |
|----------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1 | Order state machine implementation | `repo/backend/app/service/OrderService.php` (or equivalent) |
| 2 | Authorization middleware and policies | `repo/backend/app/middleware/` and `repo/backend/app/policy/` |
| 3 | API route definitions | `repo/backend/route/api.php` |
| 4 | Signed URL generation and validation | `repo/backend/app/service/MediaService.php` |
| 5 | Content moderation engine | `repo/backend/app/service/ModerationService.php` |
| 6 | Test suites for Tier 1 risks | `repo/unit_tests/order/`, `repo/unit_tests/auth/`, `repo/unit_tests/media/` |
| 7 | Database schema | `repo/backend/database/migrations/` |

## 2. Critical Checkpoints

### 2.1 Order Lifecycle Integrity

**What to verify:**
- [ ] State transitions match the documented state machine (design.md Section 13)
- [ ] `pending_match` auto-expires at exactly 30 minutes (not approximately)
- [ ] Free cancellation window is exactly 5 minutes after acceptance
- [ ] Cancellation is completely blocked when status is `in_progress`
- [ ] Dispute can only be opened within 72 hours of completion
- [ ] Disputes require Administrator resolution
- [ ] Every transition that should be blocked returns an informative error message
- [ ] The auto-expire cron command exists and is documented

**Red flags:**
- Soft enforcement (warnings instead of hard blocks)
- Missing edge cases (e.g., what happens at exactly the boundary time?)
- State transitions that don't create audit log entries

### 2.2 Authentication and Token Security

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Passwords stored as bcrypt or argon2 hashes, never plaintext
- [ ] API tokens stored as SHA-256 hashes; plaintext shown only once at creation
- [ ] Token rotation invalidates the old token immediately
- [ ] Default 90-day expiry is enforced
- [ ] Expired sessions and tokens are rejected with 401
- [ ] Token secrets never appear in logs or API responses (except the one-time creation response)

**Red flags:**
- Plaintext token storage
- Tokens in URL query parameters (visible in logs)
- Missing expiry check on token validation

### 2.3 Authorization Model

**What to verify:**
- [ ] RBAC middleware applied to every protected route
- [ ] Object-level ownership checks on listing edit/delete, order view/act, review edit/delete
- [ ] Admin-only endpoints (governance, audit, org settings, user management) reject non-admin users
- [ ] Moderator endpoints reject regular users
- [ ] Media access controlled via signed URLs (not just authentication)

**Red flags:**
- Routes missing middleware
- Ownership checks only in controllers (should be in middleware or policy layer)
- Any endpoint accessible without authentication that should require it

### 2.4 Offline-First Constraint

**What to verify:**
- [ ] No CDN links in HTML/CSS/JS files (search for `cdn.`, `googleapis.com`, `cloudflare.com`, `unpkg.com`, `jsdelivr.net`)
- [ ] No remote font imports (search for `fonts.googleapis.com`, `@import url(http`)
- [ ] No external API calls in backend code (search for `curl`, `file_get_contents('http`, `Guzzle`)
- [ ] Layui assets are vendored locally in `repo/frontend/layui/`
- [ ] All PHP dependencies are in `repo/backend/vendor/` (or Composer lock ensures offline install)
- [ ] Search and typo correction use local algorithms, not external services
- [ ] Docker Compose uses only standard base images that can be pre-pulled

**Red flags:**
- Any `https://` URL in frontend assets
- External service dependency hidden in a Composer package
- CDN fallback patterns (load local, but try CDN first)

### 2.5 Content Moderation

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Sensitive-word list is a local file, not an API call
- [ ] Rate limit (3 reviews/hour) is enforced server-side, not just client-side
- [ ] Duplicate detection uses local text similarity (trigram Jaccard) and file fingerprint (SHA-256)
- [ ] Credibility score formula matches design.md Section 14.4
- [ ] Flagged items go to manual queue (not auto-rejected or auto-published)
- [ ] Moderation actions are audit-logged

**Red flags:**
- Rate limit only on frontend
- Auto-approval of flagged content
- Credibility score used to auto-delete reviews without human review

### 2.6 Media Security

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Signed URL signature uses HMAC-SHA256 with server-side secret
- [ ] URL expiry is validated (default 10 minutes)
- [ ] Hotlink protection checks Referer header against allowed domains
- [ ] Upload validation enforces file size limits (5 MB photo, 50 MB video, 5 files max)
- [ ] MIME type is validated server-side (not just by file extension)
- [ ] File paths use UUIDs, not user-supplied filenames (prevents path traversal)

**Red flags:**
- Signature secret hardcoded in source code
- File extension used as sole type validation
- User-supplied filename used in disk path

### 2.7 Data Governance

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Behavior events are captured for browse, click, favorite, rate actions
- [ ] Nightly jobs exist as ThinkPHP commands
- [ ] Each job creates lineage records
- [ ] Quality metrics are computed and stored
- [ ] Governance dashboard only accessible to Administrators

### 2.8 PII and Logging

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Audit logs mask user_id and ip_address by default
- [ ] Unmasked view requires explicit `audit.read_unmasked` permission
- [ ] Application logs mask email, phone, IP
- [ ] Password hashes never appear in any log
- [ ] Token secrets never appear in any log

## 3. Document Consistency Checks

| Check | Files to Compare |
|-------|-----------------|
| API endpoints match route definitions | `docs/api-spec.md` vs `docs/route-inventory.md` vs actual route files |
| Screen spec covers all pages in route inventory | `docs/screen-spec.md` vs `docs/route-inventory.md` |
| Design doc models match database schema | `docs/design.md` vs migration files |
| Test coverage addresses all Tier 1 risks | `docs/test-coverage.md` vs actual test files |
| Questions.md decisions are reflected in design | `docs/questions.md` vs `docs/design.md` |
| .env.example has all referenced config keys | `docs/design.md` references vs `.env.example` |

## 4. Quick Grep Commands for Static Review

```bash
# Find any external URLs in frontend
grep -rn "https\?://" repo/frontend/ --include="*.html" --include="*.js" --include="*.css"

# Find any missing auth middleware on API routes
grep -n "Route::" repo/backend/route/api.php | grep -v "middleware"

# Find plaintext password handling
grep -rni "password" repo/backend/ --include="*.php" | grep -v "hash\|bcrypt\|argon"

# Find hardcoded secrets
grep -rni "secret\|key\|token" repo/backend/ --include="*.php" | grep -v "env(\|config(\|\.env"

# Verify all status transitions reference the state machine
grep -rn "status.*=" repo/backend/ --include="*.php" | grep -i "pending\|accepted\|in_progress\|completed\|canceled\|disputed"
```

## 5. Session Logs

Review `sessions/*.json` to verify that:
- Each phase logs files changed, requirements addressed, and open risks
- Decisions have rationale
- Open risks are tracked and addressed in subsequent sessions
"}

## Purpose

This document guides a static reviewer to the most important evidence in the repository. It identifies where to look, what to verify, and which risks need the closest attention.

---

## 1. Where to Start

| Priority | What to Review | Where to Find It |
|----------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1 | Order state machine implementation | `repo/backend/app/service/OrderService.php` (or equivalent) |
| 2 | Authorization middleware and policies | `repo/backend/app/middleware/` and `repo/backend/app/policy/` |
| 3 | API route definitions | `repo/backend/route/api.php` |
| 4 | Signed URL generation and validation | `repo/backend/app/service/MediaService.php` |
| 5 | Content moderation engine | `repo/backend/app/service/ModerationService.php` |
| 6 | Test suites for Tier 1 risks | `repo/unit_tests/order/`, `repo/unit_tests/auth/`, `repo/unit_tests/media/` |
| 7 | Database schema | `repo/backend/database/migrations/` |

## 2. Critical Checkpoints

### 2.1 Order Lifecycle Integrity

**What to verify:**
- [ ] State transitions match the documented state machine (design.md Section 13)
- [ ] `pending_match` auto-expires at exactly 30 minutes (not approximately)
- [ ] Free cancellation window is exactly 5 minutes after acceptance
- [ ] Cancellation is completely blocked when status is `in_progress`
- [ ] Dispute can only be opened within 72 hours of completion
- [ ] Disputes require Administrator resolution
- [ ] Every transition that should be blocked returns an informative error message
- [ ] The auto-expire cron command exists and is documented

**Red flags:**
- Soft enforcement (warnings instead of hard blocks)
- Missing edge cases (e.g., what happens at exactly the boundary time?)
- State transitions that don't create audit log entries

### 2.2 Authentication and Token Security

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Passwords stored as bcrypt or argon2 hashes, never plaintext
- [ ] API tokens stored as SHA-256 hashes; plaintext shown only once at creation
- [ ] Token rotation invalidates the old token immediately
- [ ] Default 90-day expiry is enforced
- [ ] Expired sessions and tokens are rejected with 401
- [ ] Token secrets never appear in logs or API responses (except the one-time creation response)

**Red flags:**
- Plaintext token storage
- Tokens in URL query parameters (visible in logs)
- Missing expiry check on token validation

### 2.3 Authorization Model

**What to verify:**
- [ ] RBAC middleware applied to every protected route
- [ ] Object-level ownership checks on listing edit/delete, order view/act, review edit/delete
- [ ] Admin-only endpoints (governance, audit, org settings, user management) reject non-admin users
- [ ] Moderator endpoints reject regular users
- [ ] Media access controlled via signed URLs (not just authentication)

**Red flags:**
- Routes missing middleware
- Ownership checks only in controllers (should be in middleware or policy layer)
- Any endpoint accessible without authentication that should require it

### 2.4 Offline-First Constraint

**What to verify:**
- [ ] No CDN links in HTML/CSS/JS files (search for `cdn.`, `googleapis.com`, `cloudflare.com`, `unpkg.com`, `jsdelivr.net`)
- [ ] No remote font imports (search for `fonts.googleapis.com`, `@import url(http`)
- [ ] No external API calls in backend code (search for `curl`, `file_get_contents('http`, `Guzzle`)
- [ ] Layui assets are vendored locally in `repo/frontend/layui/`
- [ ] All PHP dependencies are in `repo/backend/vendor/` (or Composer lock ensures offline install)
- [ ] Search and typo correction use local algorithms, not external services
- [ ] Docker Compose uses only standard base images that can be pre-pulled

**Red flags:**
- Any `https://` URL in frontend assets
- External service dependency hidden in a Composer package
- CDN fallback patterns (load local, but try CDN first)

### 2.5 Content Moderation

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Sensitive-word list is a local file, not an API call
- [ ] Rate limit (3 reviews/hour) is enforced server-side, not just client-side
- [ ] Duplicate detection uses local text similarity (trigram Jaccard) and file fingerprint (SHA-256)
- [ ] Credibility score formula matches design.md Section 14.4
- [ ] Flagged items go to manual queue (not auto-rejected or auto-published)
- [ ] Moderation actions are audit-logged

**Red flags:**
- Rate limit only on frontend
- Auto-approval of flagged content
- Credibility score used to auto-delete reviews without human review

### 2.6 Media Security

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Signed URL signature uses HMAC-SHA256 with server-side secret
- [ ] URL expiry is validated (default 10 minutes)
- [ ] Hotlink protection checks Referer header against allowed domains
- [ ] Upload validation enforces file size limits (5 MB photo, 50 MB video, 5 files max)
- [ ] MIME type is validated server-side (not just by file extension)
- [ ] File paths use UUIDs, not user-supplied filenames (prevents path traversal)

**Red flags:**
- Signature secret hardcoded in source code
- File extension used as sole type validation
- User-supplied filename used in disk path

### 2.7 Data Governance

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Behavior events are captured for browse, click, favorite, rate actions
- [ ] Nightly jobs exist as ThinkPHP commands
- [ ] Each job creates lineage records
- [ ] Quality metrics are computed and stored
- [ ] Governance dashboard only accessible to Administrators

### 2.8 PII and Logging

**What to verify:**
- [ ] Audit logs mask user_id and ip_address by default
- [ ] Unmasked view requires explicit `audit.read_unmasked` permission
- [ ] Application logs mask email, phone, IP
- [ ] Password hashes never appear in any log
- [ ] Token secrets never appear in any log

## 3. Document Consistency Checks

| Check | Files to Compare |
|-------|-----------------|
| API endpoints match route definitions | `docs/api-spec.md` vs `docs/route-inventory.md` vs actual route files |
| Screen spec covers all pages in route inventory | `docs/screen-spec.md` vs `docs/route-inventory.md` |
| Design doc models match database schema | `docs/design.md` vs migration files |
| Test coverage addresses all Tier 1 risks | `docs/test-coverage.md` vs actual test files |
| Questions.md decisions are reflected in design | `docs/questions.md` vs `docs/design.md` |
| .env.example has all referenced config keys | `docs/design.md` references vs `.env.example` |

## 4. Quick Grep Commands for Static Review

```bash
# Find any external URLs in frontend
grep -rn "https\?://" repo/frontend/ --include="*.html" --include="*.js" --include="*.css"

# Find any missing auth middleware on API routes
grep -n "Route::" repo/backend/route/api.php | grep -v "middleware"

# Find plaintext password handling
grep -rni "password" repo/backend/ --include="*.php" | grep -v "hash\|bcrypt\|argon"

# Find hardcoded secrets
grep -rni "secret\|key\|token" repo/backend/ --include="*.php" | grep -v "env(\|config(\|\.env"

# Verify all status transitions reference the state machine
grep -rn "status.*=" repo/backend/ --include="*.php" | grep -i "pending\|accepted\|in_progress\|completed\|canceled\|disputed"
```

## 5. Session Logs

Review `sessions/*.json` to verify that:
- Each phase logs files changed, requirements addressed, and open risks
- Decisions have rationale
- Open risks are tracked and addressed in subsequent sessions
"}

## 1. GO / NO-GO

**GO.** All structure, documentation, and source-of-truth files are in place. No application code written. Ready for static review and Phase 1 implementation.

---

## 2. Exact Package Tree Created

---

## 3. Extracted Core Business Requirements

1. **User registration/login** within a private organization (invite-code gated)
2. **Trip request listings** with: pickup/dropoff addresses, rider count (1-6), vehicle type (sedan/SUV/van), baggage notes, time window (12-hour clock), tags
3. **Listing workflow**: draft, publish/unpublish, edit with version history, bulk close, status badges (active/matched/in_progress/completed/canceled/disputed)
4. **Search**: keyword across title/description/tags, sort by newest/most discussed/most popular, term highlighting, vehicle type and rider count filters, keyword suggestions, on-device recent history, did-you-mean for typos, no-results fallback with recent active listings
5. **Order lifecycle**: pending_match (30-min auto-expire) -> accepted -> in_progress -> completed; cancellation free within 5 min, reason-code after, blocked in_progress; disputes within 72 hours, admin-resolved
6. **Reviews/UGC**: 1-5 stars, up to 1000 chars, photo (5MB) / video (50MB) uploads, max 5 files, preview
7. **Content moderation**: local sensitive-word filter, rate limit (3 reviews/user/hour), text similarity duplicate detection, file fingerprint dedup, credibility score (account age 14-day threshold, completion history, anomaly patterns), manual moderation queue
8. **Media**: local disk storage, signed time-limited URLs (10-min default), HMAC-SHA256 signatures, watermark overlay on images, hotlink protection (signature + referrer validation)
9. **RBAC**: resource-granularity permissions for listings, orders, reviews, moderation, governance, audit, org settings, API access; session login + API tokens (90-day expiry, rotation)
10. **Data governance**: browse/click/favorite/rate event capture, nightly dedup + missing-value fill, lineage per cleanup step, quality metrics dashboard for admins
11. **Audit/logging**: PII masking by default, unmasked only for authorized audit users, password hashes and token secrets never logged

---

## 4. Real Ambiguities (from questions.md)

| # | Ambiguity | Resolution |
|---|-----------|------------|
| Q1 | Single-org vs multi-org deployment | Schema supports multi-org via `organization_id`, but default deployment is single-org |
| Q2 | Driver vs passenger: separate roles or same user? | Same user account, behavioral distinction only; RBAC roles are separate |
| Q3 | Pending match: if driver "accepts," why pending? | Two-step: driver creates order (pending_match), passenger confirms (accepted); 30-min expiry on the pending step |
| Q4 | "Most discussed" / "most popular" undefined | Discussed = comment count; popular = favorites + views |
| Q5 | Text similarity algorithm unspecified | Trigram Jaccard, threshold 0.85, configurable, pure PHP |
| Q6 | Credibility score thresholds not specified | Configurable via .env; defaults documented; auto-flag below 0.3 |
| Q7 | Watermark: retain original? | Watermarked replaces original by default (disk space); configurable flag for retaining both |
| Q8 | Did-you-mean dictionary source | Built nightly from listing content (words with frequency >= 2); Levenshtein distance <= 2 |
| Q9 | Behavior event retention period | Default 90 days; configurable; aggregated metrics retained indefinitely |
| Q10 | Dispute resolution workflow | Outcome enum (passenger_favor/driver_favor/mutual/dismissed) + resolution text; immutable once submitted |

---

## 5. Major Static-Review Risks to Watch

| Risk | Why It Matters | Where to Verify |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Order state machine correctness** | Incorrect transitions = trust/safety failure. Boundary conditions (exactly 30 min, 5 min, 72 hours) are easy to get wrong. | `OrderService` + `OrderStateMachineTest` |
| **Authorization gaps** | A single unprotected route = privilege escalation. Object-level ownership checks are easy to miss. | Every route in `route/api.php` must have middleware; policy classes must check ownership |
| **Offline constraint violations** | A single CDN link or external API call breaks the core promise. | Grep all frontend assets for `https://`; grep backend for `curl`/`file_get_contents('http` |
| **Token/password security** | Plaintext storage or logging = catastrophic. | Verify bcrypt/argon2 for passwords, SHA-256 for token storage, no secrets in logs |
| **Signed URL bypass** | Broken signature validation = unauthorized media access. | `MediaService` signature generation/validation + tests |
| **Rate limit enforcement** | Client-only rate limits are trivially bypassed. | Must be server-side in middleware, not just frontend |
| **Moderation auto-actions** | If flagged content is auto-published or auto-rejected (instead of queued), the moderation promise is broken. | Verify all flag paths lead to the manual queue |

content.text:
1→# RideCircle Carpool Marketplace -- Design Document
     2→
     3→## 1. Business Goal
     4→
     5→Provide a fully offline, private-community carpool coordination platform where passengers post trip requests and drivers accept them, with complete lifecycle management, ratings, content moderation, and data governance -- all running on local infrastructure without any internet dependency.
     6→
     7→## 2. User Roles
     8→
     9→| Role          | Description |
    10→|---------------|-------------|
    11→| Regular User  | Can register, log in, post trip requests (as passenger), browse and accept requests (as driver), leave reviews, manage own profile. A single user can act as both passenger and driver. |
    12→| Moderator     | All Regular User capabilities plus: review moderation queue, approve/reject flagged content, view moderation dashboards. |
    13→| Administrator | All Moderator capabilities plus: manage organization settings, view governance dashboards, audit logs, resolve disputes, manage roles/permissions, configure system parameters. |
    14→
    15→All roles are scoped to a single organization. A user belongs to exactly one organization.
    16→
    17→## 3. Organization Boundary
    18→
    19→- Each deployment serves one organization (or a small set of organizations sharing a database with `organization_id` tenant isolation).
    20→- All data (users, listings, orders, reviews, media, events) is partitioned by `organization_id`.
    21→- Cross-organization data access is not permitted at the application layer.
    22→- Organization settings (name, allowed domains for hotlink protection, moderation thresholds) are configurable by Administrators.
    23→
    24→## 4. Architecture
    25→
    26→```
    27→+-------------------+          JSON/REST          +-------------------+
    28→|                   |  <========================> |                   |
    29→|  Layui Frontend   |        HTTP over LAN        |  ThinkPHP Backend |
    30→|  (Nginx static)   |                             |  (PHP-FPM)        |
    31→+-------------------+                             +--------+----------+
    32→                                                           |
    33→                                                  +--------v----------+
    34→                                                  |    MySQL 8.0      |
    35→                                                  |  (local server)   |
    36→                                                  +-------------------+
    37→                                                           |
    38→                                                  +--------v----------+
    39→                                                  |  Local Disk       |
    40→                                                  |  (media storage)  |
    41→                                                  +-------------------+
    42→```
    43→
    44→- **Frontend**: Static HTML/JS/CSS served by Nginx. All Layui assets bundled locally. No CDN, no remote fonts.
    45→- **Backend**: ThinkPHP application running behind PHP-FPM. Serves REST JSON API only -- no server-rendered HTML.
    46→- **Database**: MySQL 8.0 with utf8mb4 encoding.
    47→- **Media**: Local filesystem. Access via signed URLs generated by the backend.
    48→
    49→## 5. Frontend/Backend Separation
    50→
    51→- The frontend is a fully static single-page-like application. It makes AJAX requests to the backend API.
    52→- The backend never serves HTML views to end users. It only returns JSON responses.
    53→- CORS is configured to allow the frontend origin (same machine or LAN IP).
    54→- The frontend handles all routing, rendering, form validation (client-side), and UI state.
    55→- The backend handles all business logic, server-side validation, persistence, and authorization.
    56→
    57→## 6. API Contract Shape
    58→
    59→All API responses follow a consistent envelope:
    60→
    61→```json
    62→{
    63→  "code": 0,
    64→  "message": "success",
    65→  "data": { ... }
    66→}
    67→```
    68→
    69→- `code`: 0 for success, non-zero for errors (mapped to business error codes).
    70→- `message`: Human-readable message (English).
    71→- `data`: Payload object (null on error unless partial data is relevant).
    72→
    73→Error responses additionally include:
    74→
    75→```json
    76→{
    77→  "code": 40001,
    78→  "message": "Validation failed",
    79→  "data": null,
    80→  "errors": {
    81→    "rider_count": ["Rider count must be between 1 and 6"]
    82→  }
    83→}
    84→```
    85→
    86→HTTP status codes are used correctly (200, 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500).
    87→
    88→## 7. Auth Model
    89→
    90→### 7.1 Session Login
    91→- Users authenticate via `POST /api/auth/login` with email/password.
    92→- Backend creates a server-side session and returns a session cookie.
    93→- Session lifetime is configurable (default: 120 minutes).
    94→- Sessions are stored in the database (not filesystem) for reliability.
    95→
    96→### 7.2 API Token
    97→- Users can generate long-lived API tokens via `POST /api/auth/tokens`.
    98→- Tokens are sent in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
    99→- Default expiry: 90 days.
   100→- Token rotation: users can rotate a token (new token issued, old one invalidated) via `POST /api/auth/tokens/{id}/rotate`.
   101→- Tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes in the database. The plaintext is shown only once at creation.
   102→- Each user can have multiple active tokens (e.g., for different integrations).
   103→
   104→## 8. Session and API Token Model
   105→
   106→### Database: `user_sessions`
   107→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   108→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   109→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   110→| user_id      | BIGINT FK    | References users.id |
   111→| session_id   | VARCHAR(128) | PHP session ID |
   112→| ip_address   | VARCHAR(45)  | Client IP |
   113→| user_agent   | VARCHAR(512) | Client user agent |
   114→| last_active  | DATETIME     | Last activity timestamp |
   115→| expires_at   | DATETIME     | Session expiry |
   116→| created_at   | DATETIME     | Creation timestamp |
   117→
   118→### Database: `api_tokens`
   119→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   120→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   121→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   122→| user_id      | BIGINT FK    | References users.id |
   123→| name         | VARCHAR(100) | User-assigned name |
   124→| token_hash   | VARCHAR(64)  | SHA-256 hash of token |
   125→| last_used_at | DATETIME     | Last usage timestamp |
   126→| expires_at   | DATETIME     | Expiry (default: creation + 90 days) |
   127→| revoked_at   | DATETIME     | Null if active |
   128→| created_at   | DATETIME     | Creation timestamp |
   129→
   130→## 9. RBAC Model
   131→
   132→### Roles Table
   133→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   134→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   135→| id           | INT PK       | Auto-increment |
   136→| org_id       | BIGINT FK    | Organization scope |
   137→| name         | VARCHAR(50)  | Role name (admin, moderator, user) |
   138→| description  | VARCHAR(255) | Human-readable description |
   139→
   140→### Permissions Table
   141→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   142→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   143→| id           | INT PK       | Auto-increment |
   144→| resource     | VARCHAR(50)  | Resource type (listing, order, review, moderation, governance, audit, org_settings, api_token) |
   145→| action       | VARCHAR(50)  | Action (create, read, update, delete, publish, moderate, resolve_dispute, view_dashboard, export) |
   146→
   147→### Role-Permission Pivot: `role_permissions`
   148→Maps roles to permissions. Seeded at deployment with default grants:
   149→
   150→- **Regular User**: listing.create, listing.read, listing.update (own), listing.delete (own), order.create, order.read (own), order.update (own), review.create, review.read, media.upload, api_token.create (own), api_token.read (own), api_token.revoke (own)
   151→- **Moderator**: all Regular User permissions + moderation.read, moderation.update, review.moderate, listing.moderate
   152→- **Administrator**: all Moderator permissions + governance.view_dashboard, audit.read, org_settings.update, role.manage, dispute.resolve, user.manage
   153→
   154→### User-Role Pivot: `user_roles`
   155→| Column  | Type      | Description |
   156→|---------|-----------|-------------|
   157→| user_id | BIGINT FK | References users.id |
   158→| role_id | INT FK    | References roles.id |
   159→
   160→A user can have multiple roles. Effective permissions are the union of all assigned role permissions.
   161→
   162→## 10. Object-Level Authorization Model
   163→
   164→Beyond RBAC, resource-level ownership checks are enforced:
   165→
   166→- **Listings**: Only the creator can edit/delete their own listing. Moderators can moderate any listing. Administrators can manage all.
   167→- **Orders**: Only the passenger (listing creator) and the matched driver can view/act on an order. Administrators can view all orders.
   168→- **Reviews**: Only the author can edit/delete their own review (before moderation). Moderators can hide/remove any review.
   169→- **Media**: Media inherits access control from its parent resource (review, listing). Signed URLs are scoped to the requesting user's permissions.
   170→- **Moderation Queue**: Only Moderators and Administrators can access.
   171→- **Governance Dashboards**: Only Administrators can access.
   172→- **Audit Logs**: Only Administrators with explicit `audit.read` permission can access. Masked by default; unmasked view requires `audit.read_unmasked` permission.
   173→
   174→Implementation: ThinkPHP middleware checks RBAC first, then a policy class checks object ownership.
   175→
   176→## 11. Listing Workflow
   177→
   178→### States
   179→```
   180→[draft] --> [active] --> [matched] --> [in_progress] --> [completed]
   181→   |           |            |              |                  |
   182→   |           v            v              v                  v
   183→   |       [canceled]   [canceled]     [canceled*]       [disputed]
   184→   |                                                         |
   185→   v                                                         v
   186→[deleted]                                               [resolved]
   187→```
   188→
   189→*Cancellation from in_progress is blocked at the order level; listing stays in_progress.
   190→
   191→### Transitions
   192→| From        | To          | Trigger | Conditions |
   193→|-------------|-------------|---------|------------|
   194→| draft       | active      | Publish | All required fields valid |
   195→| draft       | deleted     | Delete  | Owner only |
   196→| active      | draft       | Unpublish | Owner or Moderator |
   197→| active      | matched     | Driver accepts | Order created |
   198→| active      | canceled    | Owner cancels or bulk close | No active order |
   199→| matched     | in_progress | Either party starts trip | Order state = accepted |
   200→| matched     | canceled    | Cancellation rules apply | Via order cancellation |
   201→| in_progress | completed   | Trip completed | Both parties or driver marks complete |
   202→| completed   | disputed    | Dispute opened | Within 72-hour window |
   203→| disputed    | resolved    | Admin resolves | Administrator action |
   204→
   205→### Bulk Close
   206→- Administrators can select multiple active listings and close them as "canceled" with reason "outdated".
   207→- Only active listings with no matched order can be bulk closed.
   208→
   209→## 12. Version History Model
   210→
   211→### Database: `listing_versions`
   212→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   213→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   214→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   215→| listing_id   | BIGINT FK    | References listings.id |
   216→| version      | INT          | Sequential version number |
   217→| snapshot     | JSON         | Full listing fields at this version |
   218→| change_summary | TEXT       | Auto-generated diff summary |
   219→| created_by   | BIGINT FK    | User who made the change |
   220→| created_at   | DATETIME     | Timestamp |
   221→
   222→- A new version is created on every edit (including draft saves).
   223→- The current published version is the latest version where `listing.status = active`.
   224→- Users can view the version history and compare any two versions (diff view).
   225→- Re-publishing after edit shows the diff to the user for confirmation.
   226→
   227→## 13. Order Lifecycle
   228→
   229→### States
   230→```
   231→[pending_match] --> [accepted] --> [in_progress] --> [completed]
   232→       |                |               |                 |
   233→       v                v               |                 v
   234→  [expired]        [canceled]           |            [disputed]
   235→                                        |                 |
   236→                                   (blocked)              v
   237→                                                     [resolved]
   238→```
   239→
   240→### State Machine Rules
   241→
   242→| Transition            | Conditions |
   243→|-----------------------|------------|
   244→| pending_match -> expired | 30 minutes elapsed with no driver acceptance (auto, via scheduled task) |
   245→| pending_match -> accepted | Driver accepts the listing |
   246→| accepted -> in_progress | Either party marks trip as started |
   247→| accepted -> canceled | Within 5 minutes of acceptance: free cancel (no reason required). After 5 minutes: reason code required. |
   248→| in_progress -> completed | Driver or both parties mark trip complete |
   249→| in_progress -> canceled | **BLOCKED** -- cancellation not allowed once in progress |
   250→| completed -> disputed | Within 72 hours of completion timestamp |
   251→| disputed -> resolved | Administrator resolves with resolution notes |
   252→
   253→### Auto-Expiry Implementation
   254→- A scheduled command (`php think order:expire`) runs every minute via cron.
   255→- Finds all orders with `status = pending_match` and `created_at < NOW() - 30 minutes`.
   256→- Transitions them to `expired` and restores the listing to `active`.
   257→
   258→### Cancellation Reason Codes
   259→| Code | Description |
   260→|------|-------------|
   261→| DRIVER_UNAVAILABLE | Driver can no longer make the trip |
   262→| PASSENGER_CHANGED_PLANS | Passenger no longer needs the ride |
   263→| VEHICLE_ISSUE | Vehicle problem prevents the trip |
   264→| SCHEDULE_CONFLICT | Time conflict arose |
   265→| OTHER | Free-text reason required |
   266→
   267→## 14. Moderation Engine
   268→
   269→### Components
   270→
   271→1. **Sensitive-Word Filter**
   272→   - A local text file contains the word list (shipped with deployment, editable by Admins).
   273→   - Applied to: review text, listing titles, listing descriptions, user display names.
   274→   - Matching: case-insensitive substring match with word-boundary awareness.
   275→   - Action: flag for manual review; content is not published until approved.
   276→
   277→2. **Rate Limiting**
   278→   - Max 3 reviews per user per hour (sliding window).
   279→   - Enforced at API layer. Returns HTTP 429 with retry-after header.
   280→
   281→3. **Duplicate Detection**
   282→   - **Text**: N-gram similarity (trigram Jaccard index) between new review text and recent reviews by the same user. Threshold configurable (default: 0.85).
   283→   - **Files**: SHA-256 fingerprint of uploaded files. Exact-match detection against all files uploaded by the same user in the last 30 days.
   284→   - Action: flag for manual review if duplicate detected.
   285→
   286→4. **Credibility Score**
   287→   - Computed per-review to weight its influence on aggregate ratings.
   288→   - Formula: `score = (W_age * age_factor) + (W_completion * completion_factor) + (W_pattern * pattern_factor)`
   289→   - **age_factor**: 0.5 if account < 14 days old, 1.0 otherwise.
   290→   - **completion_factor**: `completed_orders / total_orders` (0 if no orders).
   291→   - **pattern_factor**: 1.0 baseline, reduced by 0.25 for each anomaly (e.g., all-5-star reviews in burst, review immediately after order without plausible trip time).
   292→   - Weights configurable via `.env` (default: 0.3, 0.4, 0.3).
   293→   - Score range: 0.0 to 1.0. Reviews with score < 0.3 are auto-flagged.
   294→
   295→5. **Manual Moderation Queue**
   296→   - Flagged items appear in a queue visible to Moderators and Administrators.
   297→   - Actions: approve (publish), reject (hide with reason), escalate to Administrator.
   298→   - Queue shows: item type, content preview, flag reason, credibility score, user history summary.
   299→
   300→## 15. Duplicate Detection Approach
   301→
   302→Two-layer strategy, both fully local:
   303→
   304→### Text Similarity
   305→- Extract character trigrams from the review text.
   306→- Compute Jaccard similarity coefficient against recent reviews (same user, last 30 days).
   307→- If similarity >= threshold (default 0.85), flag as potential duplicate.
   308→- Implementation: PHP function, no external library required.
   309→
   310→### File Fingerprint
   311→- On upload, compute SHA-256 hash of file contents.
   312→- Store hash in `media.file_hash` column.
   313→- Before accepting a new upload, check for existing files with the same hash (same user, last 30 days).
   314→- If match found, flag and optionally reject with "duplicate file" message.
   315→
   316→## 16. Credibility Score Model
   317→
   318→See Section 14.4 for the formula. Additional details:
   319→
   320→### Storage
   321→- Computed at review-write time and stored in `reviews.credibility_score`.
   322→- Recomputed nightly for all reviews during governance jobs (accounts age, completion stats change).
   323→
   324→### Usage
   325→- Weighted average rating: `SUM(rating * credibility_score) / SUM(credibility_score)` per listing/driver.
   326→- Displayed to users as the listing/driver rating (not the raw average).
   327→- Administrators can view raw vs. weighted ratings on governance dashboards.
   328→
   329→### Anomaly Patterns
   330→| Pattern | Penalty |
   331→|---------|---------|
   332→| 3+ five-star reviews from same user within 1 hour | -0.25 per review in burst |
   333→| Review posted within 5 minutes of order completion | -0.25 (implausible trip time) |
   334→| Identical rating and near-identical text across multiple reviews | -0.25 |
   335→
   336→## 17. Media Storage and Signed URL Model
   337→
   338→### Storage
   339→- All media files are stored on local disk under `MEDIA_STORAGE_PATH`.
   340→- Directory structure: `/{org_id}/{year}/{month}/{day}/{uuid}.{ext}`
   341→- Original filename is stored in the database but not used for disk storage (prevents path traversal).
   342→
   343→### Database: `media`
   344→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   345→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   346→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   347→| org_id       | BIGINT FK    | Organization |
   348→| user_id      | BIGINT FK    | Uploader |
   349→| parent_type  | VARCHAR(30)  | Polymorphic: review, listing |
   350→| parent_id    | BIGINT       | Parent record ID |
   351→| file_name    | VARCHAR(255) | Original filename |
   352→| file_path    | VARCHAR(512) | Disk path relative to storage root |
   353→| file_hash    | VARCHAR(64)  | SHA-256 hash |
   354→| file_size    | BIGINT       | Size in bytes |
   355→| mime_type    | VARCHAR(100) | MIME type |
   356→| file_type    | ENUM         | photo, video |
   357→| watermarked  | BOOLEAN      | Whether watermark has been applied |
   358→| created_at   | DATETIME     | Upload timestamp |
   359→
   360→### Signed URL Generation
   361→- URL format: `/api/media/{id}?signature={sig}&expires={ts}`
   362→- Signature: `HMAC-SHA256(media_id + expires_timestamp, MEDIA_SIGN_SECRET)`
   363→- Default expiry: 10 minutes from generation.
   364→- Backend validates signature and expiry on every media request.
   365→
   366→### Upload Validation
   367→| File Type | Max Size | Allowed MIME Types |
   368→|-----------|----------|--------------------|
   369→| Photo     | 5 MB     | image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp |
   370→| Video     | 50 MB    | video/mp4, video/webm |
   371→| Per review | Max 5 files total | Mixed photo+video allowed |
   372→
   373→## 18. Watermark Model
   374→
   375→- **When**: Applied on upload if `MEDIA_WATERMARK_ENABLED=true`.
   376→- **What**: A semi-transparent PNG overlay positioned at the bottom-right corner of images.
   377→- **How**: PHP GD library (`imagecopy` with alpha blending) or Imagick if available.
   378→- **Scope**: Photos only (not videos).
   379→- **Storage**: The watermarked version replaces the original on disk. The original is not retained (to save disk space in offline deployments).
   380→- **Configurable**: Watermark image path, position, and opacity via `.env`.
   381→
   382→## 19. Hotlink Protection Model
   383→
   384→- **Signed URL Validation**: Every media request must include a valid, non-expired signature (see Section 17).
   385→- **Referrer Check**: The `Referer` header must match one of the allowed domains in `HOTLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS`.
   386→- **Enforcement**: ThinkPHP middleware checks both conditions. Requests failing either check receive HTTP 403.
   387→- **Fallback**: If the `Referer` header is missing (e.g., direct browser navigation), the signed URL alone is sufficient -- but the URL's short expiry (10 minutes) limits abuse.
   388→
   389→## 20. Data Governance Model
   390→
   391→### Event Capture
   392→All user interactions generate behavioral events stored in `behavior_events`:
   393→
   394→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   395→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   396→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   397→| org_id       | BIGINT FK    | Organization |
   398→| user_id      | BIGINT FK    | Acting user (nullable for anonymous browse) |
   399→| event_type   | VARCHAR(30)  | browse, click, favorite, rate, search, listing_view |
   400→| target_type  | VARCHAR(30)  | listing, order, review, user |
   401→| target_id    | BIGINT       | Target record ID |
   402→| metadata     | JSON         | Additional event data (search query, filter params, etc.) |
   403→| created_at   | DATETIME     | Event timestamp |
   404→
   405→Events are written asynchronously (queued in-memory, flushed in batch) to minimize request latency impact.
   406→
   407→### Listing Metadata
   408→Listing records include governance-relevant fields:
   409→- `view_count`, `favorite_count`, `click_count` (denormalized counters, updated from events).
   410→- `last_activity_at` (latest event timestamp for this listing).
   411→- `data_quality_score` (computed nightly, see Section 23).
   412→
   413→## 21. Nightly Jobs Model
   414→
   415→Scheduled via cron, implemented as ThinkPHP console commands:
   416→
   417→| Command | Schedule | Description |
   418→|---------|----------|-------------|
   419→| `php think governance:dedup` | 2:00 AM | Deduplicate behavior events (identical user+type+target within 1-minute window) |
   420→| `php think governance:fill` | 2:15 AM | Fill missing values in listing metadata (e.g., compute counters from events if denormalized counters drifted) |
   421→| `php think governance:lineage` | 2:30 AM | Record lineage entries for each cleanup step executed |
   422→| `php think governance:quality` | 2:45 AM | Compute data quality metrics for Administrator dashboard |
   423→| `php think credibility:recompute` | 3:00 AM | Recompute credibility scores for all reviews |
   424→| `php think order:expire` | Every minute | Auto-expire pending_match orders past 30 minutes |
   425→
   426→## 22. Lineage Model
   427→
   428→### Database: `data_lineage`
   429→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   430→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   431→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   432→| org_id       | BIGINT FK    | Organization |
   433→| job_name     | VARCHAR(50)  | Nightly job identifier |
   434→| run_id       | VARCHAR(36)  | UUID for this job run |
   435→| step         | VARCHAR(100) | Cleanup step name (e.g., "dedup_behavior_events") |
   436→| input_count  | INT          | Records examined |
   437→| output_count | INT          | Records after cleanup |
   438→| removed_count| INT          | Records removed/merged |
   439→| details      | JSON         | Step-specific metadata |
   440→| executed_at  | DATETIME     | Step execution timestamp |
   441→
   442→- Each nightly job run gets a unique `run_id`.
   443→- Each step within the job creates a lineage record.
   444→- Administrators can trace what happened to data at each stage.
   445→
   446→## 23. Quality Metrics Model
   447→
   448→### Database: `data_quality_metrics`
   449→| Column         | Type         | Description |
   450→|----------------|--------------|-------------|
   451→| id             | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   452→| org_id         | BIGINT FK    | Organization |
   453→| metric_date    | DATE         | Date of computation |
   454→| metric_name    | VARCHAR(50)  | Metric identifier |
   455→| metric_value   | DECIMAL(10,4)| Metric value |
   456→| details        | JSON         | Breakdown details |
   457→| computed_at    | DATETIME     | Computation timestamp |
   458→
   459→### Metrics Tracked
   460→| Metric | Description |
   461→|--------|-------------|
   462→| listing_completeness | % of listings with all optional fields filled |
   463→| event_dedup_ratio | % of duplicate events removed in nightly job |
   464→| missing_value_rate | % of records with null required-adjacent fields |
   465→| counter_drift | Max divergence between denormalized counters and event-sourced counts |
   466→| review_credibility_distribution | Histogram of credibility scores (min, p25, median, p75, max) |
   467→| moderation_queue_depth | Number of items pending in moderation queue |
   468→| stale_listing_rate | % of active listings with no activity in 7+ days |
   469→
   470→### Dashboard
   471→- Viewable by Administrators at `/admin/governance/quality`.
   472→- Shows time-series charts (last 30 days) for each metric.
   473→- Alerts when metrics cross configurable thresholds.
   474→
   475→## 24. Audit and Logging Model
   476→
   477→### Audit Log Table: `audit_logs`
   478→| Column       | Type         | Description |
   479→|--------------|--------------|-------------|
   480→| id           | BIGINT PK    | Auto-increment |
   481→| org_id       | BIGINT FK    | Organization |
   482→| user_id      | BIGINT FK    | Acting user |
   483→| action       | VARCHAR(50)  | Action performed |
   484→| resource_type| VARCHAR(50)  | Resource type affected |
   485→| resource_id  | BIGINT       | Resource ID |
   486→| old_value    | JSON         | Previous state (nullable) |
   487→| new_value    | JSON         | New state (nullable) |
   488→| ip_address   | VARCHAR(45)  | Client IP |
   489→| created_at   | DATETIME     | Timestamp |
   490→
   491→### PII Masking
   492→- By default, `user_id` and `ip_address` are masked in log views (e.g., `user_***42`, `192.168.***.***`).
   493→- Administrators with `audit.read_unmasked` permission see full values.
   494→- Password hashes and token secrets are **never** included in audit log values.
   495→
   496→### Application Logs
   497→- ThinkPHP's built-in logging writes to local files.
   498→- Log levels: debug, info, warning, error.
   499→- PII masking is applied at the log-write layer: email addresses, phone numbers, and IPs are masked unless the log consumer has audit authorization.
   500→- Log rotation: daily files, retained for 90 days.
   501→
   502→## 25. Deployment Model
   503→
   504→### Docker Compose (Primary)
   505→- Three containers: MySQL, Backend (PHP-FPM + Nginx sidecar or combined), Frontend (Nginx).
   506→- All containers on a single Docker network.
   507→- Volumes for MySQL data persistence and media storage.
   508→- `.env` file configures all runtime parameters.
   509→
   510→### Manual Deployment (Alternative)
   511→- Install PHP 8.x, MySQL 8.0, Nginx on same machine.
   512→- Configure Nginx to serve frontend static files and proxy `/api/*` to PHP-FPM.
   513→- Set up cron jobs for nightly governance commands and per-minute order expiry.
   514→
   515→### No Internet Required
   516→- All PHP dependencies vendored in `backend/vendor/` (committed or packaged).
   517→- All Layui assets in `frontend/layui/` (committed).
   518→- No Composer or npm install required at deployment time.
   519→- MySQL image can be pre-pulled or loaded from a local tarball.
   520→

1→# RideCircle Carpool Marketplace -- API Specification 2→ 3→## Conventions 4→ 5→- Base path: `/api` 6→- Content-Type: `application/json` for all requests and responses 7→- Authentication: session cookie or `Authorization: Bearer ` header 8→- All responses use the envelope: `{ "code": 0, "message": "success", "data": {...} }` 9→- Error responses add `"errors": {...}` for field-level validation failures 10→- Timestamps are ISO 8601 format (UTC) 11→- Pagination: `?page=1&per_page=20` (defaults), response includes `"meta": { "total", "page", "per_page", "last_page" }` 12→ 13→## Error Code Ranges 14→ 15→| Range | Category | 16→|-------------|----------| 17→| 0 | Success | 18→| 40001-40099 | Validation errors | 19→| 40101-40199 | Authentication errors | 20→| 40301-40399 | Authorization errors | 21→| 40401-40499 | Not found errors | 22→| 40901-40999 | Conflict / state errors | 23→| 42201-42299 | Unprocessable entity | 24→| 42901-42999 | Rate limit errors | 25→| 50001-50099 | Server errors | 26→ 27→--- 28→ 29→## 1. Authentication 30→ 31→### POST /api/auth/register 32→ 33→Register a new user account. 34→ 35→**Request:** 36→```json 37→{ 38→ "name": "string, required, 2-100 chars", 39→ "email": "string, required, valid email, unique per org", 40→ "password": "string, required, 8-72 chars, mixed case + digit", 41→ "password_confirmation": "string, required, must match password", 42→ "organization_code": "string, required, valid org invite code" 43→} 44→``` 45→ 46→**Success (201):** 47→```json 48→{ 49→ "code": 0, 50→ "message": "Registration successful", 51→ "data": { 52→ "user": { 53→ "id": 1, 54→ "name": "John Doe", 55→ "email": "john@example.local", 56→ "organization_id": 1, 57→ "roles": ["user"], 58→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T10:00:00Z" 59→ } 60→ } 61→} 62→``` 63→ 64→**Errors:** 65→- 40001: Validation failed (field errors in `errors` object) 66→- 40901: Email already registered 67→ 68→### POST /api/auth/login 69→ 70→**Request:** 71→```json 72→{ 73→ "email": "string, required", 74→ "password": "string, required" 75→} 76→``` 77→ 78→**Success (200):** 79→```json 80→{ 81→ "code": 0, 82→ "message": "Login successful", 83→ "data": { 84→ "user": { 85→ "id": 1, 86→ "name": "John Doe", 87→ "email": "john@example.local", 88→ "organization_id": 1, 89→ "roles": ["user"] 90→ }, 91→ "session_expires_at": "2026-04-08T12:00:00Z" 92→ } 93→} 94→``` 95→ 96→**Errors:** 97→- 40101: Invalid credentials 98→ 99→### POST /api/auth/logout 100→ 101→**Success (200):** 102→```json 103→{ "code": 0, "message": "Logged out", "data": null } 104→``` 105→ 106→### GET /api/auth/me 107→ 108→Returns current authenticated user profile. 109→ 110→**Success (200):** 111→```json 112→{ 113→ "code": 0, 114→ "message": "success", 115→ "data": { 116→ "user": { 117→ "id": 1, 118→ "name": "John Doe", 119→ "email": "john@example.local", 120→ "organization_id": 1, 121→ "roles": ["user"], 122→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T10:00:00Z" 123→ } 124→ } 125→} 126→``` 127→ 128→--- 129→ 130→## 2. API Tokens 131→ 132→### POST /api/auth/tokens 133→ 134→Create a new API token. 135→ 136→**Request:** 137→```json 138→{ 139→ "name": "string, required, 1-100 chars", 140→ "expires_in_days": "integer, optional, default 90, max 365" 141→} 142→``` 143→ 144→**Success (201):** 145→```json 146→{ 147→ "code": 0, 148→ "message": "Token created", 149→ "data": { 150→ "token": { 151→ "id": 1, 152→ "name": "My integration", 153→ "plaintext_token": "rc_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx", 154→ "expires_at": "2026-07-07T10:00:00Z", 155→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T10:00:00Z" 156→ } 157→ } 158→} 159→``` 160→ 161→Note: `plaintext_token` is shown only in this response. It cannot be retrieved again. 162→ 163→### GET /api/auth/tokens 164→ 165→List current user's tokens (without plaintext). 166→ 167→### DELETE /api/auth/tokens/{id} 168→ 169→Revoke a token. 170→ 171→### POST /api/auth/tokens/{id}/rotate 172→ 173→Rotate a token. Returns new plaintext, old token is immediately invalidated. 174→ 175→--- 176→ 177→## 3. Listings 178→ 179→### GET /api/listings 180→ 181→Browse/search listings. 182→ 183→**Query Parameters:** 184→| Param | Type | Description | 185→|-------|------|-------------| 186→| q | string | Keyword search (title, description, tags) | 187→| status | string | Filter by status: active, matched, in_progress, completed, canceled, disputed | 188→| vehicle_type | string | Filter: sedan, suv, van | 189→| rider_count_min | int | Min riders (1-6) | 190→| rider_count_max | int | Max riders (1-6) | 191→| sort | string | newest (default), most_discussed, most_popular | 192→| page | int | Page number | 193→| per_page | int | Items per page (default 20, max 100) | 194→ 195→**Success (200):** 196→```json 197→{ 198→ "code": 0, 199→ "message": "success", 200→ "data": { 201→ "listings": [ 202→ { 203→ "id": 1, 204→ "title": "Downtown to Airport", 205→ "description": "Need a ride to the airport tomorrow morning", 206→ "pickup_address": "123 Main St", 207→ "dropoff_address": "City Airport Terminal 2", 208→ "rider_count": 2, 209→ "vehicle_type": "sedan", 210→ "baggage_notes": "One large suitcase", 211→ "time_window_start": "2026-04-09 08:00 AM", 212→ "time_window_end": "2026-04-09 09:00 AM", 213→ "status": "active", 214→ "tags": ["airport", "morning"], 215→ "highlight": { 216→ "title": "Downtown to Airport", 217→ "description": "Need a ride to the airport tomorrow morning" 218→ }, 219→ "version": 3, 220→ "view_count": 42, 221→ "favorite_count": 5, 222→ "comment_count": 3, 223→ "user": { 224→ "id": 1, 225→ "name": "John Doe" 226→ }, 227→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T10:00:00Z", 228→ "updated_at": "2026-04-08T11:30:00Z" 229→ } 230→ ], 231→ "meta": { 232→ "total": 50, 233→ "page": 1, 234→ "per_page": 20, 235→ "last_page": 3 236→ }, 237→ "suggestions": ["airport shuttle", "airport transfer"], 238→ "did_you_mean": null, 239→ "recent_active": [] 240→ } 241→} 242→``` 243→ 244→When `q` returns no results, `recent_active` is populated with up to 10 recently active listings as a fallback. 245→ 246→When `q` appears to contain a typo, `did_you_mean` contains a corrected suggestion. 247→ 248→### GET /api/listings/{id} 249→ 250→Get listing detail. 251→ 252→**Success (200):** Full listing object with all fields including version history summary. 253→ 254→### POST /api/listings 255→ 256→Create a new listing (draft or direct publish). 257→ 258→**Request:** 259→```json 260→{ 261→ "title": "string, required, 5-200 chars", 262→ "description": "string, optional, max 2000 chars", 263→ "pickup_address": "string, required, 5-500 chars", 264→ "dropoff_address": "string, required, 5-500 chars", 265→ "rider_count": "integer, required, 1-6", 266→ "vehicle_type": "string, required, enum: sedan|suv|van", 267→ "baggage_notes": "string, optional, max 500 chars", 268→ "time_window_start": "string, required, format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm AM/PM", 269→ "time_window_end": "string, required, format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm AM/PM, must be after start", 270→ "tags": "array of strings, optional, max 10 tags, each max 30 chars", 271→ "publish": "boolean, optional, default false (save as draft)" 272→} 273→``` 274→ 275→**Success (201):** 276→```json 277→{ 278→ "code": 0, 279→ "message": "Listing created", 280→ "data": { 281→ "listing": { "...full listing object..." } 282→ } 283→} 284→``` 285→ 286→**Errors:** 287→- 40001: Validation failed 288→- 40301: Not authorized (missing listing.create permission) 289→ 290→### PUT /api/listings/{id} 291→ 292→Update a listing. Creates a new version. 293→ 294→**Request:** Same fields as POST (all optional, only changed fields required). 295→ 296→**Errors:** 297→- 40301: Not the owner 298→- 40901: Cannot edit listing in current status (e.g., in_progress) 299→ 300→### POST /api/listings/{id}/publish 301→ 302→Publish a draft or re-publish after edits. 303→ 304→**Success (200):** 305→```json 306→{ 307→ "code": 0, 308→ "message": "Listing published", 309→ "data": { 310→ "listing": { "...full listing object with status: active..." } 311→ } 312→} 313→``` 314→ 315→### POST /api/listings/{id}/unpublish 316→ 317→Unpublish an active listing (return to draft). 318→ 319→### POST /api/listings/bulk-close 320→ 321→Bulk close outdated listings. 322→ 323→**Request:** 324→```json 325→{ 326→ "listing_ids": [1, 2, 3], 327→ "reason": "outdated" 328→} 329→``` 330→ 331→**Errors:** 332→- 40301: Administrator only 333→- 40901: Some listings cannot be closed (already matched/in-progress). Response lists which failed. 334→ 335→### GET /api/listings/{id}/versions 336→ 337→Get version history. 338→ 339→**Success (200):** 340→```json 341→{ 342→ "code": 0, 343→ "message": "success", 344→ "data": { 345→ "versions": [ 346→ { 347→ "version": 3, 348→ "change_summary": "Updated pickup address and time window", 349→ "created_by": { "id": 1, "name": "John Doe" }, 350→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T11:30:00Z" 351→ } 352→ ] 353→ } 354→} 355→``` 356→ 357→### GET /api/listings/{id}/versions/{version} 358→ 359→Get a specific version snapshot. 360→ 361→### GET /api/listings/{id}/versions/{v1}/diff/{v2} 362→ 363→Compare two versions. Returns field-level diff. 364→ 365→--- 366→ 367→## 4. Search Helpers 368→ 369→### GET /api/search/suggestions 370→ 371→Keyword suggestions based on existing listing content. 372→ 373→**Query:** `?q=air` (partial keyword) 374→ 375→**Success (200):** 376→```json 377→{ 378→ "code": 0, 379→ "message": "success", 380→ "data": { 381→ "suggestions": ["airport", "airport shuttle", "air conditioning"] 382→ } 383→} 384→``` 385→ 386→### GET /api/search/did-you-mean 387→ 388→Typo correction for a search query. 389→ 390→**Query:** `?q=airprot` 391→ 392→**Success (200):** 393→```json 394→{ 395→ "code": 0, 396→ "message": "success", 397→ "data": { 398→ "original": "airprot", 399→ "suggestion": "airport", 400→ "confidence": 0.92 401→ } 402→} 403→``` 404→ 405→Implementation: Levenshtein distance against a dictionary built from listing titles, descriptions, and tags. Updated nightly. 406→ 407→--- 408→ 409→## 5. Orders 410→ 411→### POST /api/orders 412→ 413→Driver accepts a listing, creating an order. 414→ 415→**Request:** 416→```json 417→{ 418→ "listing_id": "integer, required", 419→ "driver_notes": "string, optional, max 500 chars" 420→} 421→``` 422→ 423→**Success (201):** 424→```json 425→{ 426→ "code": 0, 427→ "message": "Order created", 428→ "data": { 429→ "order": { 430→ "id": 1, 431→ "listing_id": 1, 432→ "passenger_id": 1, 433→ "driver_id": 2, 434→ "status": "pending_match", 435→ "driver_notes": "I have a blue sedan", 436→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T10:00:00Z", 437→ "expires_at": "2026-04-08T10:30:00Z" 438→ } 439→ } 440→} 441→``` 442→ 443→Note: The initial status is `pending_match`. The listing creator (passenger) must accept the match for the order to move to `accepted`. 444→ 445→### GET /api/orders 446→ 447→List orders for the current user (as passenger or driver). 448→ 449→**Query Parameters:** 450→| Param | Type | Description | 451→|-------|------|-------------| 452→| role | string | Filter: passenger, driver, or all (default) | 453→| status | string | Filter by status | 454→| page, per_page | int | Pagination | 455→ 456→### GET /api/orders/{id} 457→ 458→Get order detail with current status and allowed transitions. 459→ 460→**Success (200):** 461→```json 462→{ 463→ "code": 0, 464→ "message": "success", 465→ "data": { 466→ "order": { 467→ "id": 1, 468→ "listing_id": 1, 469→ "listing": { "...summary..." }, 470→ "passenger": { "id": 1, "name": "John Doe" }, 471→ "driver": { "id": 2, "name": "Jane Smith" }, 472→ "status": "accepted", 473→ "status_badge": "Accepted", 474→ "driver_notes": "I have a blue sedan", 475→ "cancel_reason": null, 476→ "dispute_reason": null, 477→ "resolution_notes": null, 478→ "allowed_transitions": ["in_progress", "canceled"], 479→ "cancel_free_until": "2026-04-08T10:05:00Z", 480→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T10:00:00Z", 481→ "accepted_at": "2026-04-08T10:01:00Z", 482→ "completed_at": null, 483→ "disputed_at": null, 484→ "resolved_at": null 485→ } 486→ } 487→} 488→``` 489→ 490→The `allowed_transitions` array tells the frontend which action buttons to show. When an action is blocked, the response explains why. 491→ 492→### POST /api/orders/{id}/accept 493→ 494→Passenger accepts the driver match. Transitions: pending_match -> accepted. 495→ 496→### POST /api/orders/{id}/start 497→ 498→Either party marks trip as started. Transitions: accepted -> in_progress. 499→ 500→### POST /api/orders/{id}/complete 501→ 502→Driver or both parties mark trip complete. Transitions: in_progress -> completed. 503→ 504→### POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel 505→ 506→Cancel an order. 507→ 508→**Request:** 509→```json 510→{ 511→ "reason_code": "string, required if past free-cancel window, enum: DRIVER_UNAVAILABLE|PASSENGER_CHANGED_PLANS|VEHICLE_ISSUE|SCHEDULE_CONFLICT|OTHER", 512→ "reason_text": "string, required if reason_code=OTHER, max 500 chars" 513→} 514→``` 515→ 516→**Errors:** 517→- 40901: Cannot cancel -- order is in_progress. Message: "Cancellation is not allowed once a trip is in progress." 518→- 40001: Reason code required (past 5-minute free window). 519→ 520→### POST /api/orders/{id}/dispute 521→ 522→Open a dispute. 523→ 524→**Request:** 525→```json 526→{ 527→ "reason": "string, required, max 1000 chars" 528→} 529→``` 530→ 531→**Errors:** 532→- 40901: Dispute window expired (>72 hours after completion). Message: "Disputes must be opened within 72 hours of trip completion." 533→- 40901: Order not in completed status. 534→ 535→### POST /api/orders/{id}/resolve 536→ 537→Administrator resolves a dispute. 538→ 539→**Request:** 540→```json 541→{ 542→ "resolution": "string, required, max 2000 chars", 543→ "outcome": "string, required, enum: passenger_favor|driver_favor|mutual|dismissed" 544→} 545→``` 546→ 547→**Errors:** 548→- 40301: Administrator only. 549→ 550→--- 551→ 552→## 6. Reviews 553→ 554→### GET /api/reviews 555→ 556→List reviews (filterable by listing, user, or order). 557→ 558→**Query Parameters:** 559→| Param | Type | Description | 560→|-------|------|-------------| 561→| listing_id | int | Reviews for a specific listing | 562→| user_id | int | Reviews by a specific user | 563→| order_id | int | Review for a specific order | 564→| page, per_page | int | Pagination | 565→ 566→### POST /api/reviews 567→ 568→Create a review. 569→ 570→**Request (multipart/form-data):** 571→| Field | Type | Validation | 572→|-------|------|------------| 573→| order_id | integer | Required, must be a completed order involving the user | 574→| rating | integer | Required, 1-5 | 575→| text | string | Required, 1-1000 chars | 576→| media[] | file | Optional, max 5 files total | 577→ 578→File validation: 579→- Photos: max 5 MB each, allowed: jpeg, png, gif, webp 580→- Videos: max 50 MB each, allowed: mp4, webm 581→- Total files per review: max 5 582→ 583→**Success (201):** 584→```json 585→{ 586→ "code": 0, 587→ "message": "Review submitted", 588→ "data": { 589→ "review": { 590→ "id": 1, 591→ "order_id": 1, 592→ "user_id": 1, 593→ "rating": 5, 594→ "text": "Great ride, very punctual!", 595→ "credibility_score": 0.85, 596→ "status": "published", 597→ "media": [ 598→ { 599→ "id": 1, 600→ "file_type": "photo", 601→ "url": "/api/media/1?signature=xxx&expires=1712577600", 602→ "thumbnail_url": "/api/media/1/thumbnail?signature=xxx&expires=1712577600" 603→ } 604→ ], 605→ "created_at": "2026-04-08T12:00:00Z" 606→ } 607→ } 608→} 609→``` 610→ 611→**Errors:** 612→- 42901: Rate limit exceeded (3 reviews/hour). Message: "You can submit up to 3 reviews per hour. Please try again later." Headers include `Retry-After`. 613→- 40001: Validation failed. 614→- 40901: Duplicate content detected. Message: "This review appears to be a duplicate of a recent submission." 615→ 616→### PUT /api/reviews/{id} 617→ 618→Edit own review (if not yet moderated). 619→ 620→### DELETE /api/reviews/{id} 621→ 622→Delete own review. 623→ 624→--- 625→ 626→## 7. Media 627→ 628→### GET /api/media/{id} 629→ 630→Serve a media file. Requires valid signed URL parameters. 631→ 632→**Query Parameters:** 633→| Param | Type | Description | 634→|-------|------|-------------| 635→| signature | string | HMAC-SHA256 signature | 636→| expires | integer | Unix timestamp expiry | 637→ 638→**Errors:** 639→- 40301: Invalid or expired signature. 640→- 40301: Hotlink protection failed (invalid referrer). 641→ 642→### GET /api/media/{id}/thumbnail 643→ 644→Serve a photo thumbnail (generated on first request, cached on disk). 645→ 646→--- 647→ 648→## 8. Moderation (Moderator/Admin) 649→ 650→### GET /api/moderation/queue 651→ 652→List flagged items pending review. 653→ 654→**Query Parameters:** 655→| Param | Type | Description | 656→|-------|------|-------------| 657→| type | string | Filter: review, listing, all (default) | 658→| flag_reason | string | Filter by flag reason | 659→| sort | string | newest (default), lowest_credibility | 660→| page, per_page | int | Pagination | 661→ 662→**Success (200):** 663→```json 664→{ 665→ "code": 0, 666→ "message": "success", 667→ "data": { 668→ "items": [ 669→ { 670→ "id": 1, 671→ "item_type": "review", 672→ "item_id": 42, 673→ "flag_reason": "sensitive_word", 674→ "flag_details": "Matched word: 'xxx' in review text", 675→ "credibility_score": 0.35, 676→ "content_preview": "This ride was xxx...", 677→ "user": { "id": 5, "name": "User Five", "account_age_days": 3 }, 678→ "flagged_at": "2026-04-08T12:00:00Z" 679→ } 680→ ], 681→ "meta": { "total": 15, "page": 1, "per_page": 20, "last_page": 1 } 682→ } 683→} 684→``` 685→ 686→### POST /api/moderation/queue/{id}/approve 687→ 688→Approve a flagged item (publish it). 689→ 690→### POST /api/moderation/queue/{id}/reject 691→ 692→Reject a flagged item. 693→ 694→**Request:** 695→```json 696→{ 697→ "reason": "string, required, max 500 chars" 698→} 699→``` 700→ 701→### POST /api/moderation/queue/{id}/escalate 702→ 703→Escalate to Administrator (Moderator action). 704→ 705→--- 706→ 707→## 9. Data Governance (Admin) 708→ 709→### GET /api/governance/quality-metrics 710→ 711→Get data quality metrics. 712→ 713→**Query Parameters:** 714→| Param | Type | Description | 715→|-------|------|-------------| 716→| from_date | date | Start date (default: 30 days ago) | 717→| to_date | date | End date (default: today) | 718→ 719→**Success (200):** 720→```json 721→{ 722→ "code": 0, 723→ "message": "success", 724→ "data": { 725→ "metrics": [ 726→ { 727→ "metric_date": "2026-04-07", 728→ "metric_name": "listing_completeness", 729→ "metric_value": 0.87, 730→ "details": { "total_listings": 200, "complete_listings": 174 } 731→ } 732→ ] 733→ } 734→} 735→``` 736→ 737→### GET /api/governance/lineage 738→ 739→Get data lineage records. 740→ 741→**Query Parameters:** 742→| Param | Type | Description | 743→|-------|------|-------------| 744→| job_name | string | Filter by job | 745→| run_id | string | Filter by run ID | 746→| from_date | date | Start date | 747→| to_date | date | End date | 748→ 749→### GET /api/governance/events 750→ 751→Query behavior events (admin only, paginated). 752→ 753→--- 754→ 755→## 10. Audit Logs (Admin) 756→ 757→### GET /api/audit/logs 758→ 759→Query audit logs. 760→ 761→**Query Parameters:** 762→| Param | Type | Description | 763→|-------|------|-------------| 764→| user_id | int | Filter by acting user | 765→| action | string | Filter by action type | 766→| resource_type | string | Filter by resource type | 767→| from_date | datetime | Start | 768→| to_date | datetime | End | 769→| page, per_page | int | Pagination | 770→| unmask | boolean | Request unmasked PII (requires audit.read_unmasked permission) | 771→ 772→--- 773→ 774→## 11. Organization Settings (Admin) 775→ 776→### GET /api/org/settings 777→ 778→Get current organization settings. 779→ 780→### PUT /api/org/settings 781→ 782→Update organization settings. 783→ 784→**Request:** 785→```json 786→{ 787→ "name": "string, optional, 2-200 chars", 788→ "hotlink_allowed_domains": "string, optional, comma-separated domains", 789→ "moderation_duplicate_threshold": "float, optional, 0.0-1.0", 790→ "media_watermark_enabled": "boolean, optional", 791→ "media_url_expiry_minutes": "integer, optional, 1-1440" 792→} 793→``` 794→ 795→--- 796→ 797→## 12. User Management (Admin) 798→ 799→### GET /api/users 800→ 801→List organization users. 802→ 803→### GET /api/users/{id} 804→ 805→Get user detail. 806→ 807→### PUT /api/users/{id}/roles 808→ 809→Assign roles to a user. 810→ 811→**Request:** 812→```json 813→{ 814→ "roles": ["user", "moderator"] 815→} 816→``` 817→ 818→### POST /api/users/{id}/disable 819→ 820→Disable a user account. 821→ 822→### POST /api/users/{id}/enable 823→ 824→Re-enable a disabled user account. 825→

- **Namespace**: `app\` (ThinkPHP 6 standard)
